title: Windows Command Line, PowerShell Boundaries, and Client Networking · PBQ Checkpoint
summary: A support panel compares near-miss cases for windows command line, powershell boundaries, and client networking and accepts only safe, authorized, causal, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible decisions. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for safe Windows command evidence transcript. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish cmd, PowerShell, executable, built-in command, alias, script, pipeline, object, text, quoting, variable, path, privilege, and execution-policy behavior.
Choose non-destructive commands to inspect directories, files, permissions, environment, system identity, processes, services, tasks, disks, and integrity before repair.
Inspect adapter, address, DHCP, DNS, gateway, route, neighbor, proxy, VPN, profile, sharing, and firewall state with provenance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Capture command, shell, directory, identity, time, exit status, relevant output, redaction, changed case, side effects, rollback, and unresolved limits.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Command Evidence Safety, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Command Evidence Safety precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Command Evidence Safety, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Copied Windows command output lacks shell, directory, identity, time, exit status, redaction, prediction, and side-effect evidence. The quiz variation 5 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Capture command, shell, directory, identity, time, exit status, relevant output, redaction, changed case, side effects, rollback, and unresolved limits.

For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Command Evidence Safety, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Command output is trustworthy without knowing who ran it, where, when, or under which shell.
